trace.rationale: Historical data from top-level football matches shows that exact 3-3 draws are rare, occurring in less than 2% of games. The match is a high-stakes World Cup encounter, where teams often play cautiously, reducing the likelihood of high-scoring draws. Additionally, the specific scoreline of 3-3 is even less common than general draws.
Historical soccer matches rarely end 3-3; typical draw scores are lower. Given no specific team strength info, base rate for exact 3-3 is very low.
Historical soccer matches rarely end 3-3; typical scorelines are lower-scoring. Even with competitive teams, the probability is low.

Exact 3-3 draws are rare in soccer, with historical frequency around 1-2% of matches, and no specific evidence suggests this match is more likely to end in such a high-scoring tie.

Exact 3-3 draws are rare in football, with historical frequency below 2% in top-level matches. No specific evidence suggests this match is unusually high-scoring or evenly matched to increase that probability.
